title: Assign RTOS priorities by deadline
description: Set task priorities from real-time deadlines (rate-monotonic), keep high-priority tasks short, and prevent priority inversion.
body: Use when structuring tasks on a preemptive RTOS with timing requirements. Do not assign priorities by 'importance' intuition or let a high-priority task block on a low-priority one holding a resource. Actions: derive priorities from periods/deadlines (shorter period = higher priority), keep high-priority tasks brief, and use priority-inheritance mutexes for shared resources. Validation signals include a scheduling trace showing every task meets its deadline under worst-case load and no observed priority inversion. Risk boundary: a missed hard deadline can cause physical harm or data loss; require a worst-case timing analysis and escalate if any task's deadline cannot be guaranteed.
